description: The user explicitly asked about _remove_generated_work_dir in _create_work_dir_in_place (libs/mng/imbue/mng/hosts/host.py:1081): 'the _remove_generated_work_dir call in _create_work_dir_in_place is a bit weird - we should not have to do that. can you come up with a specific example where it may be necessary?' The WIP commit (6926cc780) did not address this feedback. The call and its docstring explanation remain unchanged. While the docstring describes an edge case (worktree dir reused for in-place agent), the user's question about whether this is actually necessary was not resolved.
confidence_reasoning: The user explicitly questioned this code. The commit is marked WIP, suggesting work is incomplete. The _remove_generated_work_dir call IS defensive coding for a real edge case (directory previously created as worktree, then reused in-place), but the user specifically asked for justification and hasn't confirmed they're satisfied. The scenario described in the docstring is: (1) create worktree agent A in dir X, (2) destroy agent A, (3) create in-place agent B from dir X -- dir X is still in generated_work_dirs, so GC would delete it. This IS a real scenario.
severity_reasoning: The code is defensive and handles a real edge case. The user asked about it but hasn't rejected it - they asked for a specific example. If the edge case is real (it appears to be), the code is correct. The severity is moderate because removing it could cause a real bug (GC deleting an in-place agent's work_dir), but the user's concern about code smell is valid.

description: Stale comment at libs/mng_claude/imbue/mng_claude/plugin.py:1358 still references old terminology: '# source_path=None (clone/no-git) means trust is prompted for work_dir'. Should say '(rsync/none)' instead of '(clone/no-git)' to match the new transfer mode naming. The docstrings in _ensure_no_blocking_dialogs (line 1134-1135) were already updated to use the new terminology, but this inline comment in provision() was missed.
confidence_reasoning: The comment clearly uses old terminology ('clone') that was renamed throughout the rest of the codebase. All other comments and docstrings in this file were updated.
severity_reasoning: This is a single inline comment using old terminology. It won't cause any runtime issues but could confuse developers reading the code, especially since all surrounding comments use the new terminology.

description: In libs/mng/imbue/mng/cli/create.py:1133-1134, is_rsync_enabled is set to True when transfer_mode is TransferMode.NONE: `is_rsync_enabled=bool(opts.rsync or opts.rsync_args or transfer_mode in (TransferMode.NONE, TransferMode.RSYNC))`. However, _create_work_dir_in_place (the NONE mode handler) never checks or uses is_rsync_enabled. Setting it to True for NONE mode is a no-op that could mislead readers into thinking rsync runs during in-place mode. The condition should be `transfer_mode == TransferMode.RSYNC` instead of `transfer_mode in (TransferMode.NONE, TransferMode.RSYNC)`, or include a comment explaining why NONE is included.
confidence_reasoning: I verified that _create_work_dir_in_place does not reference is_rsync_enabled anywhere. The flag is only checked in _create_work_dir_via_git_mirror (line 1150). So enabling it for NONE mode truly has no effect. However, the old code had `git is None` which mapped to the in-place case, so this preserves the old pattern. It's possible the intent is to allow future NONE-mode rsync functionality, but there's no comment explaining this.
severity_reasoning: The flag has no effect in NONE mode since _create_work_dir_in_place doesn't check it. It's misleading rather than broken. However, it could cause confusion if someone later adds rsync support to the NONE path, thinking it was intended.
